product_updates.title: Anthropic co-founder confirms the company briefed the Trump administration on Mythos
product_updates.content: In an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark explained why the company was still engaged with the U.S. government while simultaneously suing them.

product_updates.title: Max Hodak’s Science Corp. is preparing to place its first sensor in a human brain
product_updates.content: The device could help address multiple neurological conditions if it proves successful. One early use could be delivering gentle electrical stimulation to damaged brain or spinal cord cells to encourage healing.

product_updates.title: Google adds AI Skills to Chrome to help you save favorite workflows
product_updates.content: Google is adding “Skills” to Chrome, letting users save and reuse AI prompts across websites. The feature builds on Gemini’s browser integration.

product_updates.title: In just a couple weeks, StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
product_updates.content: The first StrictlyVC of the year will be coming to San Francisco before you know it. There are still a few tickets available to join us and our stacked speaker lineup April 30, so register today!

product_updates.title: Google brings its Gemini Personal Intelligence feature to India
product_updates.content: The feature lets users connect Google accounts like Gmail and Photos to get personalized answers.

product_updates.title: How vibe-coding app Anything is rebuilding after getting booted from the App Store twice
product_updates.content: Anything is planning to launch a desktop companion app to aid mobile app development after its App Store removal.

product_updates.title: OpenAI has bought AI personal finance startup Hiro
product_updates.content: The acquisition indicates a capability that OpenAI is building into ChatGPT: financial planning.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Redefining the future of software engineering
technical_breakthroughs.content: Software engineering has experienced two seismic shifts this century. First was the rise of the open source movement, which gradually made code accessible to developers and engineers everywhere. technical_breakthroughs.title: The problem with thinking you’re part Neanderthal
